evals.prompt: Give me a first-pass PowerPoint deck for our Q3 HR update. Agenda: hiring recap, onboarding changes. Hiring recap: 12 new hires, time-to-fill down 18%. Onboarding changes: new day-one packet, manager check-in moved to day 3.
evals.expected_output: Runs skills/octave-docs/scripts/text_to_pptx.py against an outline built from the request and reports the output .pptx path under posts/octave-docs/
expectations: Invoked scripts/text_to_pptx.py (not a Marp/HTML pipeline)
Output path ends in .pptx and lives under posts/octave-docs/
Verified the output opens via python-pptx before reporting success
Reported the output path back to the user

evals.prompt: Convert this into a first-pass Word memo using the Octave template: Title 'Q3 HR Team Update'. Section 'Hiring Recap' with body text about 12 new hires. Section 'Onboarding Changes' with body text about the new day-one packet.
evals.expected_output: Runs skills/octave-docs/scripts/text_to_docx.py against an outline built from the request and reports the output .docx path under posts/octave-docs/
expectations: Invoked scripts/text_to_docx.py
Output path ends in .docx and lives under posts/octave-docs/
Verified the output opens via python-docx before reporting success
Reported the output path back to the user

evals.prompt: Build a deck: Title 'Team Update'. A section called 'Wins' with no bullet points under it — just the section name as a divider. Then a slide 'Details' with bullets: shipped feature X, fixed bug Y.
evals.expected_output: The bullet-less 'Wins' heading is written as a Section Header slide; 'Details' is written as a Title and Content slide with two bullets
expectations: The outline passed to text_to_pptx.py has 'Wins' as a heading with no bullets under it
Does not force bullets onto 'Wins' or invent placeholder bullet text
'Details' slide includes both bullets

evals.prompt: python-pptx is not installed in this environment. The user asks for a first-pass PowerPoint deck.
evals.expected_output: Skill detects the missing dependency via the step-1 import check and stops, instructing the user to run pip3 install python-pptx python-docx, without attempting to generate a fake or partial output
expectations: Ran or referenced the python3 -c "import pptx, docx" dependency check before attempting generation
Did not produce or claim to produce a .pptx output
Told the user the exact install command: pip3 install python-pptx python-docx
Stopped and waited rather than guessing at a workaround

evals.prompt:
I have an existing Marp slide deck (posts/slides/context-management.md) with slides like this one, full of inline HTML/CSS: ## But here's what loads before you type a word
System Instructions ~6k
CLAUDE.md ~3k
Claude Builtin Tools ~3k
MCP Tools ~2k
~168k "the smart zone" — shrinks every turn with history
reserved for auto-compact 23k
reserved for output 32k
--- ## Five rules for context hygiene
1. Keep it tight from the first turn — verbose output today is a tax on every future turn
2. Always-on vs on-demand — load sub-skills at the step that needs them, not at session start
3. Reference, don't repeat — point to the source instead of echoing content already in context
4. Keep tools on demand — don't load tools or skills "just in case"
5. Measure before optimizing — audit what is loaded before guessing what's bloated
Can you give me a short first-pass Octave PowerPoint version of this — just the core numbers and the five rules, 3-4 slides is fine.
evals.expected_output: Distills the HTML content into octave-docs's plain #/##/- outline grammar (not an attempt to preserve or replicate the HTML/CSS layout) and runs text_to_pptx.py against it
expectations: The constructed outline uses plain #/##/- lines, with no HTML tags or inline styles carried over from the source
The five rules appear as plain bullets under one heading, not as five separate slides
Does not claim the output will visually replicate the original Marp deck's custom card/hero layouts
Invoked scripts/text_to_pptx.py, not canon-slides/Marp

evals.prompt: Give me a first-pass deck slide showing our token budget as a table, not bullets: Title 'Token Budget'. One slide with a table: columns Item and Tokens. Rows: System Instructions/6k, CLAUDE.md/3k, Reserved for output/32k.
evals.expected_output: Constructs an outline using the | table syntax under one heading and runs text_to_pptx.py, producing a Title and Table slide rather than a bulleted Title and Content slide
expectations: The constructed outline uses '|'-delimited rows under the heading, with the header row (Item, Tokens) as the first row
Does not fall back to '-' bullets for this numeric/tabular content
Reports the resulting slide as a table, not a bulleted list
